titleStmt.title: Letter from Andrew Philip Magill to William Seddall, 15 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Andrew Philip Magill

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Andrew Philip Magill (1871–1941) an Irish civil servant to William Seddall (1869-1958), an Irish solicitor. Andrew Philip Magill acted as private secretary to Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), the chief secretary for Ireland. Magill assumed the role in 1913 and continued after the resignation of Birrell in 1916. William Seddall was an Irish solicitor based in Dublin, a Unionist and well know for his participation in Dublin courts. The letter acknowledges an application made by Seddall for the vacant position of chief crown solicitor due to the death of former Chief Crown Solicitor Malachy Kelly (1850-1916). It states the letter is on behalf of Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), the chief secretary of Ireland, and that Seddall's application will be considered.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/6796/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Copy 15th April, 1916. Dear Sir, I am desired by Mr. Birrell to acknowledge the
receipt of your letter of 14th instant, applying
for appointment as Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland,
and to say that your application will be considered
before any appointment is made. Yours faithfully, (sd) APM William Seddall, Esq.,
6,Westmoreland Street,
DUBLIN.
